approach: Delete the `init` and `mode` capability from the cyberfleet CLI entirely (#225). The `.agents/cyberfleet/ship.json` marker held only `{version:1}`, its sole reader was `detectMode`, and `detectMode`'s sole caller was the `cyberfleet mode` command itself — a closed loop gating no capability. `missions`/`jump`/`pause`/`gate` never consulted it; no hook or statusline did either.

Delete the `@frozen` `init/` (4 scenarios) and `mode/` (4 scenarios) behavioral nodes from this spec. Fires the Clearance hard floor: 8 acceptance scenarios removed from a `status: implemented` spec whose spec+impl gates were both ratified by unional personally (cr `cyberfleet-mode-init`).

Delete `packages/cyberfleet/src/{mode,init}.ts` + their tests + the two CLI verbs. Fleet membership already lives in `cyberlegion unit register` -> AgentRecord, which is what `missions` enumerates (joins ship->CR on `worktree.branch`); the marker was a shadow registry with no readers.

This spec becomes descriptive-only (no behavioral nodes remain) and its charter sharpens: cyberfleet = the SDD-derived mission view + gates; cyberlegion = the sessions. `init` is deleted, not stubbed — re-add when a ship genuinely needs on-disk config.
why: leash auto-none: deletes 8 acceptance scenarios across two @frozen suites on an implemented spec, which fires the Clearance hard floor (not self-assertable). The owner (unional) holds the user channel live this session, found the defect, and directed the deletion, so every gate verdict here is human-ratified rather than agent-self-asserted. Companion shard to `.agents/specs/cyberfleet-plugin/ledger/cyberfleet-mode-pod-precondition.440ca1.jsonl` — one CR, two touched specs.

HITL-ratified live in-session (leash auto-none — Clearance hard floor, not self-assertable). CLEARANCE FLOOR: the @frozen init/ (4 scenarios) and mode/ (4 scenarios) behavioral nodes are DELETED OUTRIGHT — 8 acceptance scenarios removed from a status:implemented spec whose spec AND impl gates were both ratified by unional personally under cr cyberfleet-mode-init. Justification (#225): the capability they specified gates nothing. `cyberfleet init` wrote .agents/cyberfleet/ship.json containing only {version:1}; its sole reader was detectMode; detectMode's sole caller was the `cyberfleet mode` command itself; that command's sole callers were the two persona mode guards, deleted alongside. missions/jump/pause/gate never consulted mode, nor did any hook or statusline. Fleet membership already lives in `cyberlegion unit register` -> AgentRecord, which is what `missions` enumerates — the marker was a shadow registry with no readers. This spec is now descriptive-only (zero behavioral nodes; the missions/jump/pause/gate backfill gap is declared and unchanged). Charter sharpens: cyberfleet = the SDD-derived mission view + gates; cyberlegion = the sessions. `init` deleted rather than stubbed — re-add when a ship genuinely needs on-disk config. Basis: cold ACED spec-judge confirmed mode/init genuinely gone from the live CLI --help, zero detectMode/initShip/ship.json references remain in any .ts/.mts, and no dangling pointer at the deleted node paths. cyberfleet package 43 tests + root pnpm verify 21/21 green. Companion shard: .agents/specs/cyberfleet-plugin/ledger/cyberfleet-mode-pod-precondition.440ca1.jsonl — one CR, two touched specs.

HITL-ratified live in-session (leash auto-none — no gate self-assertable). The implementation here is a DELETION: `src/{mode,init}.ts` + their tests + the `mode`/`init` CLI verbs are gone. Cold ACED impl-judge verified against source that `mode`/`init` are genuinely absent from the live CLI `--help` (only `missions`/`jump`/`pause`/`gate` remain), that zero references to `detectMode`/`initShip`/`ship.json` survive in any `.ts`/`.mts`, and that nothing in either persona, the marketplace readme, or the website docs still invokes them. No dangling pointer at the deleted `init/`/`mode/` node paths. 43 cyberfleet tests + root `pnpm verify` 21/21 green. This spec carries no behavioral nodes to grade — it is descriptive-only by design now; the `missions`/`jump`/`pause`/`gate` backfill gap is declared and unchanged, with `missions` flagged as the highest-value backfill since it is the CLI's whole remaining reason to exist and its `hal` field is load-bearing for the Pod persona. Companion shard: .agents/specs/cyberfleet-plugin/ledger/cyberfleet-mode-pod-precondition.440ca1.jsonl — one CR, two touched specs.
